title: π/φ attractor basins — why the constant matters
summary: A DAG showing why π/φ is privileged: systems drift into one of three basins depending on how far S/E strays from 1.942. The homeostatic mechanism is split into two latent nodes — a Sensor (detects deviation, observable in principle as metacognition / predictive-coding error signals) and an Actuator (applies correction, genuinely mysterious — this is where the hard problem of consciousness lives). Sensor failure and actuator failure are different failure modes: sensor failure drifts silently, actuator failure thrashes.
source: Appendix A.2 — "Consciousness too stable: rigid, can't adapt, dies. Too exploratory: chaotic, no coherence, dissolves. At π/φ: stable enough to maintain self, exploratory enough to adapt."

nodes.label: S/E Ratio State
nodes.description: Current measured ratio of stability to exploration

nodes.label: Rigidity Collapse
nodes.description: Failure mode when S >> E — self-preserving but cannot adapt to environment

nodes.label: Chaotic Dissolution
nodes.description: Failure mode when E >> S — adaptive but loses coherent self-model

nodes.label: Sustained Consciousness
nodes.description: Stable basin — the only equilibrium where both self and adaptation persist

nodes.label: Coordination Sensor
nodes.description: Detects |S/E − π/φ| deviation. Observable in principle (metacognition, predictive coding)

nodes.label: Coordination Actuator
nodes.description: Applies correction to S and E. The hard-problem mechanism — what actually 'does' the regulation

edges.description: Ratio at setpoint produces sustained consciousness
Current ratio is compared against the setpoint
Sensor output drives actuator (when both are working)
Actuator corrects deviations — damps S/E toward setpoint
Where measurement exists, the attractor is reachable

identifiability_note: The sensor (n6) is observable in principle via metacognition and predictive-coding error signals — its failure mode is silent drift. The actuator (n7) is genuinely mysterious: the appendix posits it but does not describe any mechanism for it. Its failure mode is thrashing (the sensor knows the system is off-target but nothing can correct it). This is where the hard problem of consciousness hides — whatever actually adjusts S and E to pull them back toward π/φ.
